whatif-004 case only checks narrativeShouldMention: ["7"] (the totalPoints value) — the no-change narrative may not always mention the exact number "7". This is intentional to verify the LLM references the actual data, but could be fragile if the LLM rounds or paraphrases. Not a defect since it's a soft eval check.

The `logSummary()` method uses SLF4J format strings with `{:30s}` positional formatting — SLF4J doesn't support printf-style format specifiers; these will log the literal `{:30s}` text rather than formatted output. The JSON output is unaffected and the test passes, but the log line will look garbled. Pre-existing pattern (the PromptEvalRunner uses similar raw string logs); not a functional defect for the benchmark.

Plan 3 cross-contributes FN for SCOPE_VOLATILITY (has NOT_ACHIEVED commits but 0 scope changes). The actual benchmark metrics for SCOPE_VOL will show FN=4, Recall≈0.2, F1≈0.29 rather than the simpler values. The placeholder table (`—`) correctly defers to runtime output, so this is fine.
`logSummary()` uses printf-style placeholders (`{:30s}`, `{:>6d}`) with SLF4J, so the console formatting will not render as intended; JSON output and test behavior are unaffected.

The markdown report is a documented placeholder companion, not auto-generated from runtime output; this matches the current step instructions but still differs from a stricter “published automatically” interpretation of the broader goal.
